chapter_summary: Jesus finishes His Sermon on the Mount with four powerful lessons: don't judge others harshly; ask God for what you need; treat people the way you want to be treated; and build your life on His words like a wise builder on rock.
main_lesson: A wise person doesn't just hear Jesus' words — they build their life on them.

memory_verse.kjv: Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus gave the Golden Rule: treat other people the way you want to be treated. This one rule sums up everything God asks us to do!

parent_guide: This final chapter of the Sermon on the Mount is one of the most action-oriented sections of the Bible. The parable of the wise and foolish builders (vv24-27) is one children grasp immediately and never forget. The big idea: hearing is not enough — doing is what changes a life. Press this home gently: what is one thing from the Sermon on the Mount your family can actually do this week? Also explore the Golden Rule (v12) practically — it's the simplest test for kindness that exists.
application_for_children: It is not enough to just know what Jesus says. A wise child hears Jesus' words AND does them — building a strong life that can stand through any storm.

verses.kjv: Judge not, that ye be not judged.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Do not judge other people, or you will be judged the same way.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't judge others. God is the judge.
verses.meaning: Jesus is not saying we can never notice right and wrong — He is saying we should not appoint ourselves as harshly condemning others, especially while ignoring our own faults.
verses.prayer: God, help me not be quick to criticize others. Remind me that You are the true judge — and that I need Your grace too.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like when someone is quick to judge you? How does that help you remember this verse?
verses.family_discussion: There is a difference between noticing wrong and harshly condemning someone. How can we speak truth to each other with love instead of judgment?
verses.do_it_today: Today, when you feel like criticizing someone, stop and think of something kind about them instead.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child pointing a finger at another child, with a gentle figure representing Jesus placing a hand on the pointing child's shoulder.

verses.kjv: For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged: and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.
verses.little_bible: Because however you judge people, God will judge you the same way. The measuring stick you use for others will be used on you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You will be measured the same way you measure others.
verses.meaning: This is a principle of fairness baked into the universe by God. Harshness returns to the harsh; mercy returns to the merciful.
verses.prayer: Lord, I want to be someone who shows mercy. Help me measure others with kindness.
verses.discussion_question: If you were strict and harsh about one of your friends' mistakes, how would you feel if they were the same way to you?
verses.family_discussion: The Golden Rule appears here in a judgment context. How does being measured by our own standards help us be more understanding?
verses.do_it_today: Before correcting a sibling or friend today, ask yourself: 'Would I want to be corrected this way?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children on either side of an old-fashioned balance scale, each side weighing the same.

verses.kjv: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked, 'Why do you notice the tiny speck in your friend's eye but don't notice the big plank of wood in your own eye?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Why see the tiny speck but not the big plank in your own eye?
verses.meaning: A 'mote' is a tiny speck of dust; a 'beam' is a large wooden plank. Jesus uses vivid humor to show how ridiculous it is to focus on others' small faults while ignoring our own large ones.
verses.memory_phrase: The plank in your own eye
verses.prayer: God, before I look at other people's faults, help me look at my own heart first. Keep me humble.
verses.discussion_question: Can you see the funny picture Jesus is describing — someone walking around with a huge plank in their eye?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus uses humor to make a serious point. Why do we find it easier to see others' faults than our own?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a trusted family member: 'Is there something I do that bothers you?' Listen without defending yourself.
verses.illustration_prompt: A cartoon-style illustration of a child with a comically large plank in their eye, squinting at a tiny speck in another child's eye.

verses.kjv: Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let me pull out the mote out of thine eye; and, behold, a beam is in thine own eye?
verses.little_bible: How can you say to your friend, 'Let me fix your tiny problem,' when you have a huge problem yourself that you haven't fixed?
verses.little_reader_adaptation: How can you help others when you have a bigger problem yourself?
verses.meaning: It is hypocritical to offer to correct someone else while being blind to greater faults in yourself. Self-awareness must come before correction.
verses.prayer: Jesus, give me the honesty to see my own heart clearly before I try to fix anyone else.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time you pointed out something a friend did wrong but forgot you had done the same thing?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to work on ourselves first before we correct others? How does humility make us better helpers?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one personal habit you want to improve. Work on that before pointing out someone else's habit.
verses.illustration_prompt: One child with a plank in their eye trying to hold tweezers toward another child's eye; the second child looks puzzled.

verses.kjv: Thou hypocrite, first cast out the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother's eye.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'First fix your own problem. Once you've done that, you will be able to help your friend clearly and kindly.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: First fix your own problem. Then you can help your friend.
verses.meaning: Jesus is not saying we should never help others — He is saying do the personal work first so that you can truly see and truly help. Genuine correction requires humility and self-awareness.
verses.prayer: God, help me deal with my own heart so I can truly love and help others well.
verses.discussion_question: How does fixing your own problem first make you a better helper to others?
verses.family_discussion: This is not a reason to never correct anyone — it's a call to honesty and humility first. How can families correct each other with both honesty and love?
verses.do_it_today: Apologize to someone today for something you did wrong — before asking them to change anything.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child removing a large splinter from their own hand, then turning with clear, kind eyes to gently help a friend.

verses.kjv: Give not that which is holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus also said, 'Do not give precious, holy things to people who will not value them — they may trample what is precious and hurt you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't give precious things to those who will not value them.
verses.meaning: This is a word of wisdom about discernment. Not everyone is ready to receive or value deep spiritual truth. We should be wise about when, where, and with whom we share sacred things.
verses.prayer: God, give me wisdom to know when and how to share Your truth. Help me be both generous and wise.
verses.discussion_question: If you had something very precious, would you give it to someone who you knew would throw it in the mud?
verses.family_discussion: How can we share our faith wisely — being generous but also thoughtful about how we share precious things with people who may not be ready?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person who seems genuinely interested in learning about God. Pray specifically for them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glistening pearl necklace being carefully held back from a muddy puddle, with a wise, thoughtful child nearby.

verses.kjv: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Ask God and He will give you. Seek God and you will find Him. Knock on God's door and He will open it for you!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Ask God. Seek God. Knock — He will open!
verses.meaning: This is one of the greatest promises about prayer in the whole Bible. God is not distant or reluctant — He is a welcoming Father who wants us to come to Him.
verses.prayer: God, I am asking. I am seeking. I am knocking. Thank You for always opening the door to me.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between asking, seeking, and knocking? Can you explain each one?
verses.family_discussion: Each word — ask, seek, knock — suggests different levels of persistence and desire. How does your family pray? Do you ask, seek, and knock?
verses.do_it_today: Write down three things you want to ask God for this week. Actually ask Him for them in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful child knocking on a large, warm-glowing door with a bright light behind it, a big smile on their face.

verses.kjv: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.
verses.little_bible: Because everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who seeks will find. Everyone who knocks will have the door opened.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone!
verses.meaning: Jesus makes the promise universal and unconditional. No one who sincerely comes to God is turned away. This is the character of our heavenly Father.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, that You never turn me away. You always hear me when I call.
verses.discussion_question: Does it encourage you to know that EVERYONE who asks will receive? What does that say about God?
verses.family_discussion: This is a promise. God does not turn anyone away who truly comes to Him. How does this change how we pray?
verses.do_it_today: Pray together as a family tonight and believe that God hears every word.
verses.illustration_prompt: Children of different backgrounds all knocking on the same big door, each one smiling as the door swings open for them.

verses.kjv: Or what man is there of you, whom if his son ask bread, will he give him a stone?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'If your child asks for bread, would any father give them a rock?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: No good dad gives his child a rock for bread!
verses.meaning: Jesus uses a simple, relatable illustration to build an argument from the lesser to the greater. If human fathers — imperfect as they are — give good things, how much more will God?
verses.prayer: God, You are a good Father. You give me what I need, not what will hurt me. I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something kind your parents or a grown-up has given you when you needed it?
verses.family_discussion: God is a Father — and the best kind of father at that. How does thinking of God as Father change how you think about prayer?
verses.do_it_today: Thank your parents or guardian for something they provided for you today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A smiling father handing a round, warm loaf of bread to a small happy child.

verses.kjv: Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?
verses.little_bible: Or if a child asks for a fish, would a good father give them a snake?
verses.little_reader_adaptation: No good dad gives his child a snake for a fish!
verses.meaning: Another simple illustration. A good father would never deliberately harm his child. God, as Father, is infinitely better and more consistently good than even the best human father.
verses.memory_phrase: Not a snake, but a fish
verses.prayer: God, You never trick me or give me things that hurt me. Help me always trust Your goodness.
verses.discussion_question: What makes a father 'good'? What do the best parents you know do?
verses.family_discussion: God gives us what is truly good — sometimes that is different from what we want. Can you think of a time God gave you something better than what you asked for?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a picture of something God has given you that is truly good.
verses.illustration_prompt: A friendly illustration of a fish in one hand and a wiggly snake in the other — with a big X over the snake.

verses.kjv: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Even people who make mistakes know how to give good things to their children. Your Father in heaven is so much better than that — He will give wonderful things to everyone who asks!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God in heaven gives even better gifts than any dad!
verses.meaning: If imperfect humans give good gifts to their children, how much more will a perfect, holy, loving God give good gifts to His children who ask? The argument from lesser to greater reaches its conclusion.
verses.prayer: Heavenly Father, You are the best giver of all. Thank You for every good gift — I know they come from You.
verses.discussion_question: What is the best gift anyone has ever given you? Now imagine God can give you something even better!
verses.family_discussion: James 1:17 says every good and perfect gift is from God. List some of the good gifts God has given your family.
verses.do_it_today: Make a 'good gifts from God' list with your family — write down five things God has given you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A heavenly Father figure surrounded by golden light, arms open wide, offering beautiful gifts from above the clouds.

verses.kjv: Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.
verses.little_bible: Jesus gave us the Golden Rule: 'Treat other people exactly the way you want to be treated. This one rule sums up all of God's law!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Treat others the way you want to be treated!
verses.meaning: The Golden Rule is the positive, active form of love your neighbor. It requires imagination — stepping into another person's shoes — and initiative: doing good first, not just avoiding harm.
verses.prayer: God, help me think about how others feel before I act. Help me treat everyone the way I want to be treated.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a way to use the Golden Rule at school or with your siblings today?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says this rule sums up the whole law and the prophets. How does treating others the way we want to be treated show love to God AND neighbor?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that you would love someone to do for you — do it for someone else first.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children exchanging gifts with big smiles — each giving the other exactly what they would love to receive.

verses.kjv: Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many there be which go in thereat:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Go through the narrow gate. The wide gate and the easy road lead to destruction — and lots of people choose that path.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The wide road leads the wrong way. Choose the narrow gate!
verses.meaning: Following Jesus is not the easy, popular path. The wide road is comfortable and crowded, but it leads away from God. The narrow way requires intention and commitment.
verses.prayer: God, help me choose the right path even when it is not the popular one. Help me follow Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time when the right thing to do was harder than the easy thing?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says many choose the wide road. How do we help each other stay on the narrow path as a family?
verses.do_it_today: When you face a choice today between what is easy and what is right, choose what is right.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright narrow path leading up through a small gate toward a beautiful mountain; a wide crowded road beside it curves away into darkness.

verses.kjv: Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it.
verses.little_bible: The gate that leads to real life is narrow — and only a few people find it. But it is the only path worth taking.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The narrow way leads to life. Find it!
verses.meaning: The narrow way is demanding but life-giving. 'Few find it' is not a discouragement but a call to seek it actively. Real life — eternal life — is worth the narrow path.
verses.memory_phrase: The narrow way leads to life
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the narrow gate. Help me follow You and not just the crowd.
verses.discussion_question: If something is hard and few people find it, does that make it less worth having? Give an example.
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says He is the way, the truth, and the life (John 14:6). He IS the narrow gate. How does that change how you read this verse?
verses.do_it_today: Memorize Matthew 7:14 together as a family: 'Narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A single figure in the distance walking through a narrow but sunlit gate, with glowing green hills on the other side.

verses.kjv: Beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves.
verses.little_bible: Jesus warned, 'Watch out for false teachers! They look like gentle sheep on the outside, but inside they are dangerous wolves.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Beware! Some wolves dress like sheep.
verses.meaning: False prophets are dangerous because they are deceptive. They look harmless or even holy, but their teaching pulls people away from God. This calls for discernment.
verses.prayer: God, help me know what is true. Give me wisdom to recognize teaching that does not match Your Word.
verses.discussion_question: How can you tell the difference between a real friend and someone pretending to be your friend?
verses.family_discussion: How do we test teachers and teaching? The answer is always: does it match what the Bible says? How can you teach children to love the Bible as their test?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse from the Bible today and ask: 'Is this what my church teacher says? Good — they match!'
verses.illustration_prompt: A fluffy sheep with a wolf's shadow behind it on the wall — children nearby looking carefully and cautiously.

verses.kjv: Ye shall know them by their fruits. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You will know what people are really like by what they produce. You don't pick grapes from thorn bushes, do you?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You know a tree by its fruit!
verses.meaning: The test of a teacher — or of any person — is not their words but their fruit: their character, their lifestyle, the effect they have on others.
verses.prayer: God, help me produce good fruit — kindness, honesty, love — so people can know I belong to You.
verses.discussion_question: What 'fruit' do you think Jesus wants to see in His followers?
verses.family_discussion: Galatians 5:22-23 lists the fruit of the Spirit. How many of those fruits can your family name? Which ones are you growing?
verses.do_it_today: Look up Galatians 5:22-23 and write down the fruit of the Spirit together.
verses.illustration_prompt: A healthy fruit tree loaded with bright fruit beside a thorny bush with no fruit — a child comparing the two.

verses.kjv: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.
verses.little_bible: A healthy tree makes good fruit. A sick tree makes bad fruit. It works the same way with people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Good trees make good fruit. Sick trees make bad fruit.
verses.meaning: A person's inner life produces their outer life. Good character flows from a transformed heart. You cannot produce lasting good fruit without being rooted in God.
verses.prayer: God, grow something good inside me so my life produces good fruit for others.
verses.discussion_question: What makes a fruit tree healthy? What makes a person's heart healthy?
verses.family_discussion: How do we stay rooted in God so we produce good fruit? What are the 'roots' of a healthy Christian life?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a tree. Label the roots with things that keep you rooted in God (prayer, Bible, church, family). Label the fruit with good things you want to produce.
verses.illustration_prompt: A lush, tall fruit tree with deep visible roots labeled 'prayer,' 'God's Word,' 'family,' 'church.'

verses.kjv: A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
verses.little_bible: A healthy tree cannot produce bad fruit. A sick tree cannot produce good fruit. That is just how it works.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You can't get good fruit from a sick tree.
verses.meaning: The connection between inner character and outward life is reliable and consistent. You cannot fake good fruit permanently — eventually the true state of the tree is revealed.
verses.prayer: Lord, change me from the inside. Make my heart truly good so everything I do reflects You.
verses.discussion_question: Can you pretend to be kind for a while? Does pretending eventually wear out?
verses.family_discussion: True change starts on the inside. What is the difference between trying harder to be good and letting God change your heart?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to change one part of your heart today — not just your actions, but your desires.
verses.illustration_prompt: A split-scene illustration: a thriving tree with ripe fruit on one side; a drooping, dry tree on the other.

verses.kjv: Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.
verses.little_bible: Every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Trees that make no good fruit will be cut down.
verses.meaning: There is urgency here. Fruitlessness is not a neutral state — it has consequences. God cares deeply about what our lives produce.
verses.prayer: God, I don't want my life to be empty of good things. Fill me with Your Spirit so I produce fruit that lasts.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God cares about whether we produce good fruit?
verses.family_discussion: God is not indifferent to how we live. What gives you the most urgency to live well and produce good fruit?
verses.do_it_today: Name one 'fruit' — one good thing — you want your life to produce more of. Make a plan to start.
verses.illustration_prompt: An old dead tree being cut down while a young strong tree nearby is full of ripe fruit.

verses.kjv: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus repeated His point clearly: 'So you will recognize people — including teachers — by the fruit their lives produce.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You know people by their fruit — their actions.
verses.meaning: Jesus repeats the key test for a reason: it is important. Watch what people produce — in character, kindness, truth, and love — not just what they say.
verses.prayer: Lord, let the fruit of my life show clearly that I belong to You.
verses.discussion_question: What fruit in a person's life makes you trust them?
verses.family_discussion: What fruit does your family want to be known for? How will you grow that fruit together?
verses.do_it_today: Ask each family member: 'What fruit do you see in me?' Share honestly and kindly.
verses.illustration_prompt: A family looking at two different trees — one full of fruit, one bare — and pointing to the full one with approval.

verses.kjv: Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said something serious: 'Not everyone who calls Me Lord will enter God's kingdom — only those who actually do what My Father in heaven says.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Calling Jesus 'Lord' is not enough. We must do what God says.
verses.meaning: This is one of Jesus' most sobering warnings. Saying the right things — even calling Jesus 'Lord' — is not enough. Real faith obeys.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I don't just want to call You Lord with my mouth. I want to truly live like You are Lord of my life.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between calling someone your boss and actually doing what they say?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like in daily life to truly do God's will rather than just use Christian words?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one thing you know God wants you to do — and do it today, not just talk about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children — one shouting 'Lord! Lord!' with hands raised but walking away; one quietly serving others with a smile.

verses.kjv: Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works?
verses.little_bible: On that day, many will say, 'Lord, Lord! We spoke for You! We drove out evil! We did great things in Your name!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some will say: we did great things for You, Lord!
verses.meaning: These people did impressive religious activities — but apparently without a true relationship with Jesus. Religious activity without genuine relationship and obedience is not enough.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I don't just want to do things for You. I want to truly know You and walk with You every day.
verses.discussion_question: Can someone do 'Christian' things without really knowing Jesus? What is the difference?
verses.family_discussion: Relationship with Jesus is the foundation. How does your family nurture a real, personal relationship with Jesus — not just religious habits?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes talking to Jesus today like He is your best friend — not a prayer list, just a conversation.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of people showing trophies and certificates of their religious works to a figure of Jesus, who looks at them with sadness.

verses.kjv: And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus will say to them, 'I never truly knew you. Go away from Me — you were living the wrong way.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus will say: I never knew you. What matters is knowing Jesus.
verses.meaning: The most terrifying words in the Bible. The core issue is not what we did but whether Jesus truly knows us and we truly know Him in personal, obedient relationship.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want You to know me — really know me. And I want to really know You. Help me walk close to You.
verses.discussion_question: How do you get to know someone well? How do you get to know Jesus well?
verses.family_discussion: How does your family help each child build a real, personal relationship with Jesus — not just learn Bible facts?
verses.do_it_today: Write a short letter to Jesus telling Him you want Him to know you and you want to know Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child writing a letter with the words 'Dear Jesus' at the top, sitting in warm lamplight with a look of earnestness.

verses.kjv: Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'If you hear My words AND put them into practice, you are like a wise builder who builds his house on solid rock.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hear Jesus' words AND do them — build on rock!
verses.meaning: This is the hinge verse of the parable. Hearing is not enough. The wise person is defined by doing. Building on rock = hearing + obeying Jesus' teaching.
verses.memory_phrase: Hear and do — build on rock
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me not just hear Your words but truly do them. I want to build my life on You.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between hearing something and actually doing it?
verses.family_discussion: Which teachings from the Sermon on the Mount (Matthew 5-7) can your family start doing this week?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one teaching from Matthew 5-7 and plan together how to practice it this week as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A cheerful builder in a construction hat laying the foundation of a house on solid grey rock, tools and blueprints nearby.

verses.kjv: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.
verses.little_bible: The rain poured down, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat hard against that house — but it did not fall! It was built on solid rock.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The storm came — but the rock house stood firm!
verses.meaning: The storms of life are coming. Jesus does not promise a storm-free life — He promises a storm-proof foundation. Life built on His words holds when everything shakes.
verses.memory_phrase: Built on rock, it stood firm
verses.prayer: Jesus, be my rock. When hard things come, I want to be standing because I am built on You.
verses.discussion_question: What kinds of 'storms' do people face in life? (hard times, sadness, scary things, trouble)
verses.family_discussion: Jesus promises that building on His words makes us storm-proof. What storms has your family faced? How did faith in Jesus help?
verses.do_it_today: Build something together — even with toy blocks — and talk about what makes a strong foundation.
verses.illustration_prompt: A solid stone house standing firm in a dramatic rainstorm with lightning in the sky; rain bouncing off the roof while the house glows warmly inside.

verses.kjv: And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:
verses.little_bible: But if you hear Jesus' words and do NOT do them, you are like a foolish builder who builds his house on sand.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If you hear but don't do — you build on sand!
verses.meaning: The foolish builder is not someone who never heard — he heard the same words. The difference is response. Hearing without doing produces a dangerous, unstable life.
verses.memory_phrase: Hear but don't do — build on sand
verses.prayer: God, please help me never just be a hearer. Turn every truth I hear into something I live.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think building on sand is so dangerous? (Sand moves and washes away!)
verses.family_discussion: What are some 'sand' foundations people build their lives on? (popularity, money, their own feelings) How do they compare to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Try building a sandcastle in a bucket and pouring water on it. Watch what happens. Talk about what it means.
verses.illustration_prompt: A cheerful but naive builder hammering stakes into soft beach sand, unaware of dark clouds gathering on the horizon.

verses.kjv: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.
verses.little_bible: The rain came down, the floods rose, and the winds beat against that house — and it fell down with a great crash!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The storm came — and the sand house crashed!
verses.meaning: The collapse is total — 'great was the fall of it.' A life built on anything other than Jesus cannot hold when the storms come. The parable ends with urgency: choose wisely now.
verses.memory_phrase: Great was the fall of it
verses.prayer: Jesus, I choose to build my life on You. You are the rock that will never move.
verses.discussion_question: How does this story make you feel? Does it make you want to make sure you are building on rock?
verses.family_discussion: The crash is dramatic and complete. How does this parable motivate your family to be doers of the Word, not just hearers?
verses.do_it_today: Together, make a simple family commitment: 'We will not just hear — we will do.' Write it on your fridge.
verses.illustration_prompt: A house on a sandy shore collapsing dramatically in a storm, waves washing it away, walls tumbling.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended these sayings, the people were astonished at his doctrine:
verses.little_bible: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowd was amazed at His teaching. They had never heard anything like it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone was amazed at how Jesus taught!
verses.meaning: The crowd's reaction to the Sermon on the Mount was astonishment. Jesus' teaching was different from anything they had heard — it had life, authority, and power.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I read Your words, fill me with wonder. Let me never get bored of what You say.
verses.discussion_question: Has a Bible verse ever amazed you or surprised you? Which one?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd was astonished by Jesus' teaching. When was the last time a Bible passage truly amazed your family? Read it together again.
verses.do_it_today: Find one Bible verse that amazes you and share it with the family at dinner.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd of people sitting on a hillside, mouths open in wonder, eyes wide, leaning forward as Jesus finishes speaking.

verses.kjv: For he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
verses.little_bible: Jesus did not teach like the religious teachers who said 'the ancient teachers wrote...' Jesus spoke with His own authority. He spoke as God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus taught with real authority — unlike anyone else!
verses.meaning: The scribes always cited other authorities: 'Rabbi so-and-so said...' Jesus said 'I say to you' — speaking in His own name. This is the authority of God Himself, and the crowd felt it.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your words have real power. Help me take them seriously and let them change my life.
verses.discussion_question: What makes someone's words feel powerful and trustworthy?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus speaks as God — with true authority over every part of our lives. How should that change the way we read the Bible?
verses.do_it_today: Read one passage of Jesus' teaching and ask: 'What is Jesus actually commanding here? How can I obey it?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a hillside at sunset, speaking with calm authority to a large attentive crowd below; golden light around Him.
